content:
RORK — FINAL ENTERPRISE OWNER SIGN-IN ROOT AUDIT AND PERMANENT FIX

STOP ALL OTHER TASKS.

The IVX app now opens, but the Owner AI screen displays:

“This account is not the IVX owner. Sign in with the owner account.”

This has been happening repeatedly for approximately one month.

Do not add another temporary bypass.
Do not hardcode owner access.
Do not hide the error.
Do not ask me to create another account.
Do not claim this is fixed until the complete owner flow is verified on a clean Android installation.

MISSION

Audit every owner-authentication and owner-authorization root one by one, repair the existing architecture, deploy matching code, build a matching APK, and prove that the correct owner account consistently receives owner access.

The required result is:

1. Correct owner credentials sign in.
2. Owner role is verified from the production database.
3. IVX Owner AI becomes usable.
4. Owner Control becomes usable.
5. Non-owner accounts remain denied.
6. Logout works.
7. Reopening the application does not produce the wrong owner state.
8. No automatic sign-in to the wrong account.
9. No stale cached session.
10. No mismatch between mobile, backend and Supabase environments.

────────────────────────────────────
PHASE 1 — CAPTURE THE CURRENT AUTH STATE
────────────────────────────────────

On the exact screen showing:

“This account is not the IVX owner”

capture and return:

- authenticated user ID
- authenticated email, partially masked
- authenticated phone, partially masked
- session present: YES or NO
- access token present: YES or NO
- Supabase project reference
- auth environment
- profile table queried
- role field queried
- returned role
- returned active status
- returned owner status
- authorization decision
- exact rejection reason
- trace ID

Never expose passwords, access tokens, refresh tokens or secret keys.

Return user identifiers only in masked or sanitized form.

────────────────────────────────────
PHASE 2 — IDENTIFY THE EXACT OWNER AUTHORIZATION SOURCE
────────────────────────────────────

Audit the actual existing repository and identify:

- active AuthProvider
- active auth context file
- active Supabase client
- active owner verification function
- owner login screen
- Owner AI route
- Owner Control route
- admin route guard
- owner role query
- logout function
- startup session restoration
- auth-state listeners

Return:

- file
- line number
- function
- purpose
- current behavior
- expected behavior

Do not create a second OwnerAuthProvider unless the existing provider is proven unusable.

There must be one source of truth for authentication and one source of truth for authorization.

────────────────────────────────────
PHASE 3 — SEARCH EVERY OWNER CHECK
────────────────────────────────────

Search the entire repository for:

- isOwner
- owner
- owner_email
- ownerEmail
- OWNER_EMAIL
- OWNER_EMAILS
- role === "owner"
- role == "owner"
- admin
- super_admin
- active
- profiles
- users
- members
- user_roles
- auth.users
- user_metadata
- app_metadata
- email comparison
- phone comparison
- user.id comparison
- owner ID
- authorized owner
- not the IVX owner
- sign in with the owner account
- owner access denied
- owner gate
- admin gate
- useAuth
- AuthProvider
- onAuthStateChange
- getSession
- INITIAL_SESSION
- SIGNED_IN
- SIGNED_OUT
- TOKEN_REFRESHED
- AsyncStorage
- SecureStore
- cached session
- restore session
- persistSession
- router.replace
- router.push
- Redirect

For every match return:

- file
- line
- exact logic
- whether retained
- whether changed
- whether removed
- reason

Delete duplicate owner checks.

There must not be one email rule in the app, another role rule in the backend and a third rule in Supabase.

────────────────────────────────────
PHASE 4 — VERIFY THE PRODUCTION OWNER RECORD
────────────────────────────────────

Verify the correct production owner account in Supabase.

Audit all possible authorization tables:

- auth.users
- profiles
- users
- members
- user_roles
- admin_users
- owner_profiles
- team_members

Do not assume which table is correct.

Identify the canonical owner record.

The canonical record must include:

- auth user ID
- normalized email
- role
- active status
- owner authorization status
- created_at
- updated_at

Required authorization:

- authenticated user ID matches profile ID
- role = owner
- active = true

If the architecture uses a separate role table, confirm the foreign-key relationship is correct.

Check for:

- duplicate owner profiles
- duplicate emails
- old owner accounts
- deleted auth users
- profile record linked to the wrong auth ID
- capitalization differences
- trailing spaces
- phone-only account mismatch
- stale user metadata
- role value such as Owner instead of owner
- null active status
- archived profile
- different Supabase project
- test environment record instead of production record

Normalize email with:

trim().toLowerCase()

Do not authorize only by email.

The final authorization must be based on authenticated user ID plus production role verification.

────────────────────────────────────
PHASE 5 — VERIFY MOBILE AND BACKEND USE THE SAME SUPABASE PROJECT
────────────────────────────────────

Verify:

- Expo Go Supabase URL
- Android APK Supabase URL
- backend Supabase URL
- production Supabase project reference
- anon-key project binding
- service-role project binding
- auth redirect configuration

Return only:

- component
- variable name
- project reference
- PRESENT or MISSING
- match result

Never expose key values.

All production components must use the same approved Supabase project.

Fail the task if:

- APK uses a test project
- backend uses another project
- Expo Go uses another project
- role data exists in only one environment

────────────────────────────────────
PHASE 6 — FIX AUTHENTICATION STATE ORDER
────────────────────────────────────

Audit the complete sequence:

1. App starts.
2. Supabase client initializes.
3. Session is read.
4. Auth user is established.
5. Owner profile query starts.
6. Owner profile query completes.
7. Owner authorization is calculated.
8. Owner routes become available.

Required states:

- authInitializing
- unauthenticated
- authenticatedCheckingRole
- authenticatedOwner
- authenticatedNonOwner
- authError

Do not treat “profile query still loading” as “not owner.”

Do not render:

“This account is not the IVX owner”

until the owner-profile query has completed successfully.

During verification display:

“Verifying owner access…”

with a bounded timeout.

If verification fails:

- show exact sanitized error
- provide trace ID
- allow Retry
- allow Sign Out
- do not enter an infinite loop

────────────────────────────────────
PHASE 7 — REMOVE DUPLICATE LISTENERS AND STATE OVERWRITES
────────────────────────────────────

Verify that:

- onAuthStateChange is registered once
- the listener is unsubscribed once
- profile verification is not started repeatedly
- isOwner is not reset by another provider
- session is not overwritten by stale AsyncStorage state
- logout clears all related owner state
- retry does not create another listener
- app focus does not duplicate verification
- token refresh does not incorrectly set isOwner=false

Add trace logging for:

- AUTH_PROVIDER_MOUNTED
- SESSION_READ_STARTED
- SESSION_READ_COMPLETED
- AUTH_USER_IDENTIFIED
- OWNER_PROFILE_QUERY_STARTED
- OWNER_PROFILE_QUERY_COMPLETED
- OWNER_AUTHORIZED
- OWNER_DENIED
- AUTH_STATE_CHANGED
- OWNER_STATE_CHANGED
- OWNER_ROUTE_RENDERED

Every event must include:

- trace ID
- elapsed milliseconds
- sanitized user ID
- source function

────────────────────────────────────
PHASE 8 — FIX OWNER LOGIN
────────────────────────────────────

Owner login must:

1. Accept manually entered email and password.
2. Normalize the email.
3. Clear any stale non-owner session before login.
4. Call Supabase signInWithPassword once.
5. Receive authenticated user ID.
6. Query the canonical owner record by authenticated user ID.
7. Require role = owner.
8. Require active = true.
9. Set owner state only after verification succeeds.
10. Navigate to Owner Control only after owner state is confirmed.

Failure behavior:

- wrong password → visible auth error
- user not found → visible auth error
- non-owner account → signed out and denied
- inactive owner → denied
- profile missing → exact authorization error
- network failure → retry available
- 401/403 → exact sanitized response
- timeout → return to login without black screen

Never create owner access from:

- a cached email
- a local boolean
- route history
- user metadata alone
- a hardcoded account
- a development bypass

────────────────────────────────────
PHASE 9 — FIX THE OWNER AI SCREEN
────────────────────────────────────

The screen shown in the screenshot must use the verified owner state.

Required behavior:

A. Owner verified

- warning disappears
- message field enabled
- send button enabled
- attachments enabled
- voice enabled
- Live Work enabled
- Control button enabled
- owner tools available

B. Authentication still loading

- show “Verifying owner access…”
- do not show denial prematurely

C. Non-owner

- show access denied
- disable owner tools
- show Sign Out
- show Go to Owner Login

D. Authorization error

- show trace ID
- Retry verification
- Sign Out

Do not show a normal IVX Owner AI welcome message above an owner-access denial. The screen must have one consistent state.

────────────────────────────────────
PHASE 10 — ROUTE PROTECTION
────────────────────────────────────

Protect every owner route, including:

- Owner AI
- Control
- Admin
- Business Overview
- Members admin
- Investors admin
- Projects admin
- Deals admin
- Reels admin
- Revenue
- Finance
- Settings
- Diagnostics
- Variables
- Deployment controls
- Autonomous controls

Required guard:

authenticated session
AND canonical owner record exists
AND role = owner
AND active = true

Direct links and navigation history must not bypass this guard.

After logout:

- replace route with Owner Login
- clear owner state
- clear protected navigation history
- Android Back cannot reopen Owner AI or Control

────────────────────────────────────
PHASE 11 — DATABASE SECURITY
────────────────────────────────────

Verify Supabase RLS and backend authorization.

Client-side route hiding is not sufficient.

For every owner/admin table verify:

- RLS enabled
- owner policy exists
- policy uses authenticated user ID
- policy checks canonical owner role
- non-owner query is denied
- non-owner mutation is denied
- owner query succeeds
- owner mutation succeeds

Verify backend owner endpoints also validate the JWT and owner role.

Do not trust a role sent from the client.

────────────────────────────────────
PHASE 12 — CLEAN SESSION TEST
────────────────────────────────────

Perform:

1. Sign out.
2. Clear application storage.
3. Uninstall old APK.
4. Install the final APK.
5. Launch.
6. Confirm Owner Login.
7. Sign in with correct owner credentials.
8. Confirm owner profile verification.
9. Open Owner AI.
10. Confirm warning is gone.
11. Send a test message.
12. Open Control.
13. Open Admin.
14. Force-stop.
15. Reopen.
16. Confirm expected session behavior according to approved policy.
17. Sign out.
18. Press Android Back.
19. Confirm protected routes remain inaccessible.
20. Sign in with a non-owner account.
21. Confirm denial.

Do not reuse an old cached session as proof.

────────────────────────────────────
PHASE 13 — OWNER QA MATRIX
────────────────────────────────────

Run and report:

1. Correct owner email/password
2. Wrong password
3. Unknown email
4. Non-owner valid account
5. Inactive owner
6. Missing profile
7. Duplicate profile
8. Wrong role capitalization
9. Email with uppercase characters
10. Email with leading/trailing spaces
11. Session expired
12. Token refresh
13. Network offline
14. Slow network
15. Supabase timeout
16. Supabase 401
17. Supabase 403
18. App background/foreground
19. Force-stop/reopen
20. Logout
21. Android Back after logout
22. Direct deep link to Owner AI
23. Direct deep link to Control
24. Retry owner verification
25. Ten login/logout cycles
26. Ten cold launches
27. Expo Go
28. Android APK
29. Owner AI message send
30. Owner Control access

Required:

- 30/30 pass
- zero automatic wrong-account login
- zero false “not owner” result
- zero duplicate listeners
- zero infinite loaders
- zero black screens

────────────────────────────────────
PHASE 14 — ALIGN CODE, BUILD AND BACKEND
────────────────────────────────────

Final source of truth:

GitHub main branch HEAD

Required match:

- GitHub commit SHA
- APK embedded SHA
- backend deployed SHA
- /health SHA
- diagnostics SHA

Do not use labels instead of the real 40-character SHA.

If GitHub access fails:

- verify stored variable
- verify binding
- call GitHub API
- return exact status

Only ask for credential intervention after verified:

- MISSING
- 401
- 403
- EXPIRED
- REVOKED
- DELETED

────────────────────────────────────
PHASE 15 — REQUIRED FINAL EVIDENCE
────────────────────────────────────

Return:

1. exact root cause
2. authenticated user state from failed screen
3. canonical owner table
4. canonical owner record verification
5. files changed
6. lines changed
7. duplicate owner checks removed
8. duplicate listeners removed
9. production Supabase project reference
10. mobile/backend project match
11. RLS verification
12. backend authorization verification
13. 30-test QA table
14. TypeScript result
15. lint result
16. Expo Doctor result
17. Android build result
18. GitHub repository
19. branch
20. final GitHub SHA
21. APK embedded SHA
22. APK filename
23. APK link
24. APK SHA-256
25. backend deployment ID
26. backend deployed SHA
27. /health response
28. startup/auth trace
29. Android logcat
30. real-device screen recording showing:
    - logged-out state
    - owner login
    - owner verification
    - Owner AI enabled
    - test message sent
    - Control opened
    - Admin opened
    - logout
    - denied non-owner test

────────────────────────────────────
DEFINITION OF DONE
────────────────────────────────────

This task is complete only when:

- the correct owner account is recognized every time
- the current false “not the IVX owner” state is eliminated
- Owner AI works
- Control works
- Admin works
- non-owner access is denied
- logout works
- stale sessions cannot produce the wrong account
- mobile and backend use the same Supabase project
- RLS and backend authorization pass
- all SHAs match
- clean-install Android QA passes
- complete evidence is attached

Do not return another plan.

Execute the audit one root at a time.

If one blocker remains, return only:

- exact blocker
- file
- line
- service
- request
- sanitized response
- trace ID
- attempted fix
- required resolution
